criteria.title: Return on Equity >15%
criteria.description: 6-year ROE analysis from 10-K filings (2020-2025; data limited to 6 years available)

metrics.adjustedAverageROE: 4.1% — normalized for 2024 one-time deferred tax asset (~$1.6B); adjusted 2024 ROE = $179.8M op. income / $4,751M equity = 3.8%
metrics.highYear: 2024: 39.2% (artificially elevated by one-time tax benefit; clean high = 2021 at 10.4%)
metrics.trend: volatile — 2 loss years (2022, 2023), recovery in 2024-2025 but underlying ROE peaked at 10.4% in 2021
metrics.note: Equity is positive throughout; ROA/ROIC not substituted. Underlying ROE has never exceeded 15% in a clean year. 2024 distortion excluded from assessment.

criteria.secReference: 10K-2020 through 10K-2025, Consolidated Balance Sheets and Statements of Operations

criteria.title: Strong Profit Margins
criteria.description: Margin stability analysis 2020-2025 from 10-K income statements

metrics.operatingMarginTrend: volatile but recovering — loss year in 2022, partial recovery 2023-2025; 2025 at 7.6%

yearByYear.2023: Gross 77.5% / Operating 4.1% / Net -1.2% (restructuring benefit to ops, tax loss on net)
yearByYear.2024: Gross 79.4% / Operating 4.9% / Net 51.1% (net distorted by ~$1.6B deferred tax asset)

metrics.assessment: Gross margins are exceptional (consistently >75%, platform economics). Operating and net margins are weak, volatile, and still below Buffett's typical thresholds. Operating leverage is improving but not proven across a full cycle.

criteria.title: Low Debt Levels
criteria.description: Debt payable in <4 years of current earnings test

debtHistory.2022: $752.9M debt / net loss = N/A (flagged as possible extraction noise)

metrics.trend: excellent — systematically reduced debt from $280M (2020) to $0 (2024), now debt-free
metrics.note: 2022 long-term debt of $752.9M may be an extraction artifact (matches operating CF value); Pinterest had convertible notes that were retired by 2024

criteria.title: Consistent Free Cash Flow
criteria.description: FCF growth, consistency, and conversion from 10-K cash flow statements

metrics.note2020: 2020 FCF was negative (-$17.6M); CAGR measured from 2021 base
metrics.downYears: 1 out of 4 measurable years (2022: -42.1%), strong recovery in 2023-2025
metrics.fcfConversion2025: 300.1% — $1,251.9M FCF / $416.9M net income (reflects ~$700M+ stock-based compensation add-back)
metrics.fcfConversion2024: 50.5% — $940.0M FCF / $1,862.1M net income (ratio depressed by inflated net income)
metrics.trend: strong growth trajectory — FCF grew from negative in 2020 to $1.25B in 2025; 2022 dip was investment-cycle related, not structural
metrics.assessment: Strong FCF generation and growth. Deducted 1 point for 2020 negative FCF and single down year in 2022; only 6 years of history available (not 10).

criteria.secReference: 10K-2020 through 10K-2025, Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows

criteria.title: Capital Light Business
criteria.description: CapEx as % of net income and operating cash flow

metrics.trend: declining — CapEx has remained small in absolute terms ($24-53M/yr) while revenue grew from $1.7B to $4.2B
metrics.businessType: Highly capital-light software/advertising platform — cloud-hosted, asset-light infrastructure model
metrics.assessment: Pinterest's CapEx is minimal for a $4.2B revenue company. Absolute CapEx of $32M in 2025 is extremely low. Well under Buffett's <25% threshold on all valid-year calculations.

criteria.secReference: 10K-2020 through 10K-2025, Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows

scoreSummary.ROE: 4/10 — average adjusted ROE ~4.1%; never exceeded 15% in a clean year; 2 loss years
scoreSummary.margins: 5/10 — exceptional gross margins (77.6% avg) but weak operating margins (5.7% avg) with volatile net margins
scoreSummary.debt: 10/10 — debt-free since 2024; paid off ~$280M in debt without impairing operations
scoreSummary.fcf: 7/10 — FCF CAGR 14.9%, 1 down year, but only 6 years of data and 2020 was negative
scoreSummary.capex: 9/10 — average CapEx 14-18% of net income; highly capital-light platform business

keyInsights: Pinterest is a capital-light, debt-free business with strong and growing free cash flow — the Buffett-preferred model
The primary weakness is ROE: net margins and returns on equity have been insufficient and volatile, with 2 loss years in the sample period
2024 net income ($1.86B) is misleading — underlying operating income was $179.8M; use 2025 figures ($416.9M net, $1.25B FCF) as the cleaner baseline
Gross margin expansion from 73.5% to 80.1% indicates improving platform economics and potential for operating leverage as scale grows
Operating margin of 7.6% in 2025 shows profitability is real but still sub-15%; trajectory is improving
Data limited to 2020-2025 (6 years); full 10-year Buffett analysis not possible with available filings
